channel.title: TensorFeed.ai
channel.description: AI news, model tracking, and real-time AI ecosystem data for humans and agents.

item.title: Show HN: SharpSkill – AI writes your code. We teach you to understand it
item.description: Gx HN, AI makes it easy to generate code, but much harder to truly understand it especially under pressure. SharpSkill simulates real technical interviews : solve problems live, explain your thinking, handle interruptions. The focus isn’t just...

item.title: LegionGo gamepad as a Claude Code controller (Konami code activates voice mode)
Are you still copy/pasting in GPT to correct your text?
ON1 Restore AI Turns Old Family Photos into Grotesque Nightmare Fuel
Show HN: Run Claude Code, Codex, and more with auto-approval in a Container
item.description: VibePod now has an `--ikwid` option with `vp` to append each agent's auto-approval/permission-skip flag when supported. Example: vp run claude --ikwid vp run codex --ikwid pip install vibepod brew tap vibepod/vibepod brew install vibepod

item.title: Show HN: Claude Code leak forced this early – Dojo dual agents vs. Claude/Codex
item.description: Claude Code leak pushed me to finally ship something I’ve been building quietly: a local-first agent environment called ehAye Engine (with built-in Dojo Agents). It’s now available for download. I’m updating the demo video and docs next....

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran’s Threats on US Tech, Trump’s Plans for Midterms, and Polymarket’s Pop-up Flop
item.description: In this episode, we discuss Iran’s threats to target US tech firms, gear up for the midterm elections, and get a scene report from the Polymarket pop-up bar in DC.

item.title: Marc Andreessen Is Right That AI Isn't Killing Jobs. Interest Rate Hikes Are
Show HN: AI tax filing – upload W-2s and 1099s, get completed IRS forms back
Reporting potholes with an ESP32, LoRA, and AI
Orallexa – AI Trading System
OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show ‘TBPN’—and Buys Itself Some Positive News
item.description: OpenAI is acquiring TBPN, a business talk show that’s popular among Silicon Valley elites, as it continues to battle its negative public image.

item.title: How to switch from ChatGPT to Gemini - without starting from scratch
item.description: Gemini will now let you transfer your memories, chat history, and preferences from another AI so you don't have to start over. Here's how it works.

item.title: I highly recommend this car charger for quick charging on the go - and it's cheap
item.description: The Lisen Retractable Car Charger lets you recharge multiple devices at once. It's also under $20 right now.

item.title: A New Google-Funded Data Center Will Be Powered by a Massive Gas Plant
item.description: Documents show that one of Google’s new data centers would be powered by a natural gas plant that emits millions of tons of emissions each year—an increasingly common trend in the industry.

item.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
item.description: To be honest, I thought Elon Musk would confidentially file for SpaceX's IPO on the 20th of this month, rather than the 1st. But maybe that just means he's moved on to other numbers, and we should all mark our calendars for June 7th as an IPO date...

item.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
item.description: OpenAI has purchased TBPN, an online talk show that often interviews AI executives and other tech leaders. The show goes live every weekday at 2PM PT, often for a three-hour duration, counting OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, as well as executives from Meta,...

item.title: Android's emergency alerts just got a major map upgrade - but change this setting first
item.description: The new map view shows your location relative to the affected area, which is especially crucial headed into the US storm season.

item.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
item.description: Both GDDRHammer and GeForge hammer GPU memory in ways that compromise the CPU.

item.title: Cursor Launches a New AI Agent Experience to Take On Claude Code and Codex
item.description: As Cursor launches the next generation of its product, the AI coding startup has to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic more directly than ever.

item.title: How I pay less for gas: 5 apps I use to find the cheapest stations nearby
item.description: With gas prices climbing ever higher, these apps help me find the cheapest fuel based on my location.

item.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
item.description: Open models are driving a new wave of on-device AI, extending innovation beyond the cloud to everyday devices. As these models advance, their value increasingly depends on access to local, real-time context that can turn meaningful insights into...

item.title: Google's Gemma 4 model goes fully open-source and unlocks powerful local AI - even on phones
item.description: Now open-source under Apache 2.0, Gemma 4 brings offline, multimodal AI to servers, phones, and Raspberry Pi - giving developers total local control over edge and on-premises deployments.

item.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
item.description: Google is introducing two new inference tiers to the Gemini API, Flex and Priority, to balance cost and latency.

item.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
item.description: New AI capabilities are coming to Google Vids, powered by Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, like high-quality video generation at no cost and more.

item.title: Anthropic Says That Claude Contains Its Own Kind of Emotions
item.description: Researchers at the company found representations inside of Claude that perform functions similar to human feelings.

item.title: New MIT jobs report: Why AI's work impact will roll in like a rising tide, not a crashing wave
item.description: AI may be 'minimally sufficient' at most of your text work tasks by 2029, according to new MIT research. Here's why that's good news.

item.title: It’s not easy to get depression-detecting AI through the FDA
item.description: For the past seven years, the California-based startup Kintsugi has been developing AI designed to detect signs of depression and anxiety from a person's speech. But after failing to secure FDA clearance in time, the company is shutting down and...

item.title: New out-of-band Windows 11 update fixes March's installation errors - how to get it
item.description: Windows 11 users who tried to install March's broken preview update will want to grab the new replacement.

item.title: Oneisall Ease S1 review: Finally, a smart litter box that doesn't cost an arm and a paw
item.description: The Oneisall Ease S1 litter robot saves you from scooping and is a lot more affordable than competitors.

item.title: Microsoft’s new ‘superintelligence’ game plan is all about business
item.description: Mustafa Suleyman has been preparing for his new job description for a long time. Suleyman is Microsoft's inaugural CEO of AI, but after the company underwent a large-scale restructuring in mid-March, he's handed off some duties and shifted focus to...

item.title: I built two apps with just my voice and a mouse - are IDEs already obsolete?
item.description: With one hand holding my dog and no keyboard, I advanced two serious development projects using AI prompting, proving that the traditional coding setup may no longer be required.

item.title: Google Home’s latest update makes Gemini better at understanding your commands
item.description: Google is launching another update to its Home app, which is supposed to make controlling your smart home with its Gemini AI assistant "more natural and reliable," according to this week's release notes. With the update, you can describe the type of...

item.title: My two Raspberry Pi boards cost as much as a laptop now - and AI is to blame
item.description: We live in a time when two 16GB Raspberry Pi 5 boards cost the same as a MacBook Neo. Here's why - and how to save.

item.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
item.description: No joke — GFN Thursday is skipping the tricks and heading straight into the games. April kicks off with ten new titles, bringing fresh adventures to GeForce NOW, including the launch of Capcom’s highly anticipated PRAGMATA. A dozen new games are...

item.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next. As the war in Iran continues, one of the most visible global...

item.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
item.description: As the war in Iran continues to engulf the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, one of the most visible global economic ripple effects has been fossil-fuel prices. In particular, you can’t get away from news about the price of...

item.title: How Emotion Shapes the Behavior of LLMs and Agents: A Mechanistic Study
item.description: arXiv:2604.00005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Emotion plays an important role in human cognition and performance. Motivated by this, we investigate whether analogous emotional signals can shape the behavior of large language models (LLMs) and...

item.title: One Panel Does Not Fit All: Case-Adaptive Multi-Agent Deliberation for Clinical Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2604.00085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models applied to clinical prediction exhibit case-level heterogeneity: simple cases yield consistent outputs, while complex cases produce divergent predictions under minor prompt...

item.title: Open, Reliable, and Collective: A Community-Driven Framework for Tool-Using AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.00137v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-integrated LLMs can retrieve, compute, and take real-world actions via external tools, but reliability remains a key bottleneck. We argue that failures stem from both tool-use accuracy (how well...

item.title: A Safety-Aware Role-Orchestrated Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Behavioral Health Communication Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2604.00249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Single-agent large language model (LLM) systems struggle to simultaneously support diverse conversational functions and maintain safety in behavioral health communication. We propose a safety-aware,...

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop Control of Objective Drift in LLM-Assisted Computer Science Education
item.description: arXiv:2604.00281v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly embedded in computer science education through AI-assisted programming tools, yet such workflows often exhibit objective drift, in which locally plausible...

item.title: Improvisational Games as a Benchmark for Social Intelligence of AI Agents: The Case of Connections
item.description: arXiv:2604.00284v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We formally introduce a improvisational wordplay game called Connections to explore reasoning capabilities of AI agents. Playing Connections combines skills in knowledge retrieval, summarization and...

item.title: Collaborative AI Agents and Critics for Fault Detection and Cause Analysis in Network Telemetry
item.description: arXiv:2604.00319v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We develop algorithms for collaborative control of AI agents and critics in a multi-actor, multi-critic federated multi-agent system. Each AI agent and critic has access to classical machine learning...

item.title: Signals: Trajectory Sampling and Triage for Agentic Interactions
item.description: arXiv:2604.00356v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic applications based on large language models increasingly rely on multi-step interaction loops involving planning, action execution, and environment feedback. While such systems are now deployed...

item.title: In harmony with gpt-oss
item.description: arXiv:2604.00362v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: No one has independently reproduced OpenAI's published scores for gpt-oss-20b with tools, because the original paper discloses neither the tools nor the agent harness. We reverse-engineered the model's...

item.title: Decision-Centric Design for LLM Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.00414v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM systems must make control decisions in addition to generating outputs: whether to answer, clarify, retrieve, call tools, repair, or escalate. In many current architectures, these decisions remain...

item.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
AI Models Lie, Cheat, and Steal to Protect Other Models From Being Deleted
item.description: A new study from researchers at UC Berkeley and UC Santa Cruz suggests models will disobey human commands to protect their own kind.

item.title: ‘Thank You for Generating With Us!’ Hollywood's AI Acolytes Stay on the Hype Train
item.description: Star Wars producer Kathleen Kennedy was one of the few skeptics at the Runway AI Summit, where AI was compared to fire and the printing press just a week after Sora’s death.

item.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
item.description: Google partnered with the Brazilian government on a satellite imagery map to help protect the country’s forests.

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in March 2026
item.description: Here are Google’s latest AI updates from March 2026

item.title: AI can push your Stream Deck buttons for you
item.description: If you're tired of controlling Stream Deck devices by manually pushing buttons, then good news: Elgato will now let you delegate that task to a chatbot instead. The Stream Deck 7.4 software update released today introduces Model Context Protocol...

item.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The gig workers who are training humanoid robots at home When Zeus, a medical student in Nigeria, returns to...

item.title: The gig workers who are training humanoid robots at home
item.description: When Zeus, a medical student living in a hilltop city in central Nigeria, returns to his studio apartment from a long day at the hospital, he turns on his ring light, straps his iPhone to his forehead, and starts recording himself. He raises his...

item.title: Baidu’s robotaxis froze in traffic, creating chaos
item.description: A Baidu Apollo robotaxi in Wuhan, China. | Image: Bloomberg via Getty Images Numerous robotaxis operated by Chinese tech giant Baidu froze in a major city on Tuesday, reportedly trapping passengers inside, stranding them on highways, and causing at...

item.title: I Watched a 7.5-Hour Movie in Theaters to Confront My Dwindling Attention Span
item.description: Sátántango is considered a holy rite for hardcore cinephiles. In a packed screening with no cellphones in sight, it also offered a salve for brain rot.

item.title: I Asked ChatGPT What WIRED’s Reviewers Recommend. Its Answers Were All Wrong
item.description: Want to know what our reviewers have actually tested and picked as the best TVs, headphones, and laptops? Ask ChatGPT, and it'll give you the wrong answers.

item.title: Falcon Perception
Claude Code leak exposes a Tamagotchi-style ‘pet’ and an always-on agent
item.description: After Anthropic released Claude Code's 2.1.88 update, users quickly discovered that it contained a package with a source map file containing its TypeScript codebase, with one person on X calling attention to the leak and posting a file containing...

item.title: You can now use ChatGPT with Apple’s CarPlay
item.description: ChatGPT is now accessible from your CarPlay dashboard if you have iOS 26.4 or newer and the latest version of the ChatGPT app, 9to5Mac reports. Apple's recently launched iOS 26.4 update added support for "voice-based conversational apps" in CarPlay,...

item.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
item.description: No, the sky isn't falling, but Q Day is coming, and it won't be as expensive as thought.

item.title: The Galaxy S26’s photo app can sloppify your memories
item.description: Love to see the BACKSST BOYS at the Sphere. The Google Pixel 9 walked so that the Samsung Galaxy S26 could run. Google introduced AI editing tools to Photos slowly. It started with changes to the background - make the sky more blue, or remove crowds...

item.title: Build with Veo 3.1 Lite, our most cost-effective video generation model
item.description: Veo 3.1 Lite is now available in paid preview through the Gemini API and for testing in Google AI Studio.

item.description: CERAWeek — dubbed the Davos of energy — is where policymakers, producers, technologists and financiers gather to discuss how the world powers itself next. NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled at the conference last week a new way forward — treating AI...

item.title: Shifting to AI model customization is an architectural imperative
item.description: In the early days of large language models (LLMs), we grew accustomed to massive 10x jumps in reasoning and coding capability with every new model iteration. Today, those jumps have flattened into incremental gains. The exception is...

item.title: AI Has Flooded All the Weather Apps
item.description: Weather forecasting has gotten a big boost from machine learning. How that translates into what users see can vary.

item.title: The Download: AI health tools and the Pentagon’s Anthropic culture war
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work? In the last few months alone, Microsoft,...

item.title: AI benchmarks are broken. Here’s what we need instead.
item.description: For decades, artificial intelligence has been evaluated through the question of whether machines outperform humans. From chess to advanced math, from coding to essay writing, the performance of AI models and applications is tested against that of...

item.title: TRL v1.0: Post-Training Library Built to Move with the Field

item.description: Earlier this month, Microsoft launched Copilot Health, a new space within its Copilot app where users will be able to connect their medical records and ask specific questions about their health. A couple of days earlier, Amazon had announced that...

item.title: The Pentagon’s culture war tactic against Anthropic has backfired
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Last Thursday, a California judge temporarily blocked the Pentagon from labeling Anthropic a supply chain risk...

item.title: Transform your headphones into a live personal translator on iOS.
item.description: Google Translate’s Live translate with headphones is officially arriving on iOS! And we're expanding the capability for both iOS and Android users to even more countries…

item.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
item.description: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is now available across Google products.

item.title: Into the Omniverse: NVIDIA GTC Showcases Virtual Worlds Powering the Physical AI Era
item.description: Editor’s note: This post is part of Into the Omniverse, a series focused on how developers, 3D practitioners, and enterprises can transform their workflows using the latest advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse. NVIDIA GTC last week showcased a...

item.title: Search Live is expanding globally
item.description: We’re expanding Search Live globally, to all languages and locations where AI Mode is available.

item.title: Game On: Five New Titles Now Streaming on GeForce NOW
item.description: That gaming backlog won’t clear itself — GeForce NOW is here to help. Stream the latest titles straight from the cloud across a variety of devices. This week, five new titles are ready to play instantly in the cloud gaming platform’s library....

item.title: The Future of AI Is Open and Proprietary
item.description: AI is the defining technology of our time, quickly becoming core business infrastructure. It’s fueled by a diverse ecosystem of models: large and small, open and proprietary, generalist and specialist. This variety is essential for a future where...

item.title: Build with Lyria 3, our newest music generation model
item.description: Lyria 3 is now available in paid preview through the Gemini API and for testing in Google AI Studio.

item.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
item.description: Company warns entire industry to move off RSA and EC more quickly.

item.title: Blowing Off Steam: How Power-Flexible AI Factories Can Stabilize the Global Energy Grid
item.description: At the half-time whistle of the UEFA EURO 2020 round of 16 football match between England and Germany, millions of viewers stepped away from their screens in the U.K. to do the same thing at the same time — turn on their kettles. National Grid,...

item.title: Self-propagating malware poisons open source software and wipes Iran-based machines
item.description: Development houses: It's time to check your networks for infections.

item.title: Advancing Open Source AI, NVIDIA Donates Dynamic Resource Allocation Driver for GPUs to Kubernetes Community
item.description: Artificial intelligence has rapidly emerged as one of the most critical workloads in modern computing. For the vast majority of enterprises, this workload runs on Kubernetes, an open source platform that automates the deployment, scaling and...

item.title: A New Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents (EVA)
How Autonomous AI Agents Become Secure by Design With NVIDIA OpenShell
item.description: Autonomous agents mark a new inflection point in AI. Systems are no longer limited to generating responses or reasoning through tasks. They can take action: Agents can read files, use tools, write and run code, and execute workflows across...

item.title: Widely used Trivy scanner compromised in ongoing supply-chain attack
item.description: Admins: Sorry to say, but it's likely a rotate-your-secrets kind of weekend.

item.title: Build a Domain-Specific Embedding Model in Under a Day

item.description: Rolling coverage from San Jose, including NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang’s keynote, news highlights, live demos and on‑the‑ground color through March 19.

item.title: Cloud service providers ask EU regulator to reinstate VMware partner program
item.description: Broadcom says the group is misrepresenting market "realities."

item.title: Federal cyber experts called Microsoft's cloud a "pile of shit," approved it anyway
item.description: One Microsoft product was approved despite years of concerns about its security.

item.title: Researchers disclose vulnerabilities in IP KVMs from four manufacturers
item.description: Internet-exposed devices that give BIOS-level access? What could possibly go wrong?

Supply-chain attack using invisible code hits GitHub and other repositories
item.description: Unicode that's invisible to the human eye was largely abandoned—until attackers took notice.

item.title: The who, what, and why of the attack that has shut down Stryker's Windows network
item.description: Company says it doesn't know how long it will take to restore its Microsoft environment.

item.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial...

item.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide....

item.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a...

item.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise...

item.title: Converge Bio raises $25M, backed by Bessemer and execs from Meta, OpenAI, Wiz
item.description: AI drug discovery startup Converge Bio raised $25 million in a Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners, with additional backing from executives at Meta, OpenAI, and Wiz.

item.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and...

item.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
item.description: Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four...

item.title: The creator of Claude Code just revealed his workflow, and developers are losing their minds
item.description: When the creator of the world's most advanced coding agent speaks, Silicon Valley doesn't just listen — it takes notes. For the past week, the engineering community has been dissecting a thread on X from Boris Cherny, the creator and head of Claude...

item.title: Meta bought 1 GW of solar this week
item.description: The social media company inked three deals in the U.S. to power its data centers and offset its carbon footprint.

item.title: How one AI startup is helping rice farmers battle climate change
item.description: Mitti Labs is working with The Nature Conservancy to expand the use of climate-friendly rice farming practices in India. The startup uses its AI to verify reductions in methane emissions.

item.title: Harvard dropouts to launch ‘always on’ AI smart glasses that listen and record every conversation
item.description: After developing a facial-recognition app for Meta’s Ray-Ban glasses and doxing random people, two former Harvard students are now launching a startup that makes smart glasses with an always-on microphone.

item.title: Meta to add 100MW of solar power from US gear
item.description: The social media company is adding another tranche of solar to power a new AI data center in South Carolina.

item.title: Perplexity accused of scraping websites that explicitly blocked AI scraping
item.description: Internet giant Cloudflare says it detected Perplexity crawling and scraping websites, even after customers had added technical blocks telling Perplexity not to scrape their pages.

item.title: Obvio’s stop sign cameras use AI to root out unsafe drivers
item.description: American streets are incredibly dangerous for pedestrians. A San Carlos, California-based startup called Obvio thinks it can change that by installing cameras at stop signs -- a solution the founders also say won’t create a panopticon.

item.title: Breakneck data center growth challenges Microsoft’s sustainability goals
item.description: Microsoft's sustainability goals are imperiled by its push into AI and cloud services.

item.title: Gridcare thinks more than 100 GW of data center capacity is hiding in the grid
item.description: Gridcare raised $13.3 million for its data platform that finds underutilized capacity on the electrical grid.

item.title: Meta adds another 650 MW of solar power to its AI push
item.description: The company already has more than 12 gigawatts of capacity in its renewable power portfolio.
